rulings.Case Title: EST. OF JOSEPH A. GAGGERO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/2/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) When an accounting is presented, original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. None appears to have been filed. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (2) The $435 attorney cost incurred on 7/22/22 does not appear to have been for filing the Confidential Supplement to Duties and Liabilities. (This was presumably the filing fee.) Please provide a courtesy copy of the bank statement to the assigned examiner by 11/18/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHRISTINE YOUNG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following procedural issues have not been cleared: (1) The notice was filed on 10/16/24 (by Isis Jah); however, it does not set forth the date and place of mailing on Page 2, Proof of Service, #4. No phone number of the proposed personal rep on Page 1, #10. No notice of instruction of remote appearance attached. (2) Duties and Liabilities of Personal Representative [Form DE-147] was filed on 10/16/24 (by Isis Jah); however, it does not set forth the address and the phone number of the proposed administrator on Page 2, #2. (3) Petition #5.a.(3)-(4) to set forth whether the decedent was survived by a registered domestic partner or not. Amended Petition does not affirm. (4) Proposed Order to be submitted. Local Rule 14.7. (5) Amended Petition incorrectly affirms that "beneficiaries" have waived bond (#3.e.(2)) instead of "heirs" have waived bond (#3.e.(3)). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EVA SMALL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues raised by the Court at the previous three status hearings in January, May, and July 2024, despite the continuance of the last hearing (09/09/2024) granted by the assigned judicial officer without requiring counsel's fourth appearance: 1) No FINAL inventory and appraisal has been filed since the administrator received all assets from the decedent's conservator more than 18 months ago. 2) One partial inventory and appraisal was filed in January 2024, but it only lists real property (but not cash and tangible personal items identified in the conservatorship final accounting as date-of-death assets). 3) The administrator's current bond ($1,197,000) is insufficient, based on the partial inventory and appraisal value ($1,470,000) alone, but no ex parte petition has been filed to increase the bond. The Court is to consider whether the administrator should be suspended and/or whether statutory fee reduction under Probate Code section 12205 may be warranted at the hearing for final distribution or allowance of fees. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RYAN TIMOTHY HODGSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/9/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No one has been appointed to represent the legal interests of the minors. (2) A testamentary trust does not exist until the order for final distribution is signed. The presumptive trustee of the proposed testamentary trust cannot waive his own accounting. (3) The Court may consider the amount of statutory compensation when determining compensation for extraordinary services. [CRC 7.703(a).] Fee petitions before this Court usually set forth how much time was spent on ordinary services. Other than the claim filed by the (separated) wife and the requirement to file an estate tax return, for both of which matters the attorneys are seeking additional fees, the estate administration does not appear to have been very complicated, such that the $164,559.22 statutory fee should adequately cover more than the ordinary services. Furthermore, the time sheets reflect multiple billing parties conferring and working on matters; this Court does not favor internal conferences beyond the minimum required for a more senior attorney to review the work of an associate or paralegal. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 11/25/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TORU SAKAMOTO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/9/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The legal description (Exhibit A) is missing from Attachment 7a to the proposed order (not "petition"). (2) Why, if the couple both had earnings throughout their marriage, did the decedent need to take out a loan individually? (3) The Lincoln Fund appears to be a joint account. Does it not pass to the petitioner by operation of law? (4) The E-Trade account shows "TOD." Was there no designated beneficiary? Please provide a complete proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 11/25/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE SUZANNE CASTLE TRUST DATED APRIL 22, 2009
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Petition For Order To Set Aside Amendment Of Trust, And Invalidate Will Due To (1) Undue Influence, (2) Lack Of Testamentary Capacity, And (3) Elder Abuse; Remove Trustee; Compel Trustee To Provide All Trust Documents And Accounting; Impose Punitive Damages
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/4/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) A "supplement" filed on 10/14/24 appears to AMEND the petition. An amendment to a petition requires the same notice as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] (2) If the addresses are now known for those interested parties whose addresses were unknown at the time the petition was filed, a verified supplement to the petition must be filed to set forth the names and addresses of those interested parties. Please provide courtesy copies of any new papers to the assigned examiner by 11/20/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.] Issues should be addressed in a supplement or an amendment to a petition (or a completely amended petition) that stands on its own, not in response to issues posted in a tentative ruling or to "Examiner's Notes." For example: attaching a proposed order to a "Verified Response" is inappropriate; the proposed order must be lodged or, ideally, delivered to the examiner for the Court to sign. A proof of service should just be filed, not attached to a pleading describing its filing.)

rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status of closing the estate will be set for 11/3/25. The administrator must file either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. C. Section 12200 (requesting additional time for estate administration) at least two weeks before that status date. Appearance will be required on 11/3/25 if no petition has been filed.

rulings.Case Title: *TRANSFERRED TO LA COUNTY* IN THE MATTER OF THE CHRIS PRENTISS TRUST DATED JUNE 30, 1998, as amended
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For 1. Cancellation Of Instrument; 2. Court Order To Invalidate The Prematital Agreement And The Postnuptial Agreement Based On Fraud In The Inducement, Or Alternateively, As It Pertains To The Postnuptial Agreement, Based On Mutual Mistake. 3. Order Confirming Community Interest In Trust Assets Pursuant To Family Code Section 1101(B); 4. Return Of Property Held By Trust Pursuant To Probate Code Section 850; And 5. Financial Elder Abuse.
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/16/2024. No response to notes sent 085/23/2024.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE NAOMI RUTH PIERCE LIVING TRUST, DTD APRIL 1, 2002
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Bring Trust Under Court Supervision
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/25/2024, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 08/14/2024): 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) A copy of the trust was not attached to the petition. 3) Notice must be given to the court-appointed attorney for the beneficiary/conservatee, absent a signed waiver. 4) Please provide an itemized list of assets currently by the trustee, including fair market value of each asset. 5) Please provide estimated annual gross income of the trust. 6) The trustee is subject to the same terms and conditions as a conservator of the estate, unless specifically waived by the Court upon a showing of good cause. See LRSF 14.47(B)(5). Other than the bond, is the trustee requesting waiver of any requirement applicable to a conservator of the estate? Please clarify the extent of court supervision sought by the petition and identify each trust provision affected by the proposed supervision.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE HIROKAZU SHINOHARA REVOCABLE TRUST DATED TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Property
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/4/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The trustee resides in Utah. Does he also have a residence in San Francisco? (2) The order directed the assets to be distributed to this decedent's ESTATE. (Unfortunately, the order was issued without proof of a personal representative having been appointed for the estate of Hirokazu Shinohara.) Who are this decedent's heirs-at-law? They are entitled to personal service of notice for an 850 petition. [Prob. C. Sect. 17203(b) and CCP Sect. 413.10 et seq.] Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 11/20/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DOMINEY FAMILY 2000 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Compel Purported Trustee To Distribute California Real Property To Beneficiaries
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/9/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) If the trustee (purported or otherwise) resides in Oregon, and a case is pending there, San Francisco does not appear to be the proper venue. (2) If Judith Henn died on 1/31/24, who is her successor-in-interest? (Has a personal representative been appointed for her estate, or did she have a trust?) Such person is entitled to notice. (3) Article Two, Section D. of the Second Amendment and Restatement does not explicitly exclude from the "heirs" the otherwise disinherited daughter Deborah A. Henn; she would arguably be entitled to a share of the trust now. If the petition is not withdrawn or dismissed, please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 11/25/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANTOINETTA G RITGER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.
